01 — About

Engineer by instinct,
builder by trade

Design has been my obsession since childhood — sketching logos, pulling objects apart, always asking what makes something well made. That curiosity grew from graphic design into 3D modelling, then mechanical CAD, then PCB layout — each field a deeper version of the same question.

Today I build across circuit boards, CAD assemblies, engineering drawings, and software — freelancing on Upwork and Fiverr with a consistent 5-star record. I hold a BS in Graphic Design (GPA 3.96) and a Google Cybersecurity Certificate. The designer's eye goes into every board. The security training goes into every line of networked code.

PCB Design
Upwork · 2024 · Motor Control

VectorDrive X4

Multi-Channel Motor Driver — Production-Ready PCB

A fully routed, production-ready multi-channel motor driver PCB featuring LM358 op-amps and PS4231 MOSFETs. Includes a 3-sheet schematic with complete trace routing in Altium Designer — ERC/DRC clean and ready for fabrication. Delivered with full BOM and Gerber package to client.

Fiverr · 2024 · Microcontroller Systems

NexusBoard M1

Microcontroller Motor Controller — 4-Layer KiCad PCB

A microcontroller-based motor controller PCB with a complex multi-IC schematic and a KiCad 4-layer board layout. The board spans 205 nodes across 47 nets, demonstrating advanced placement strategy and signal integrity planning. Designed with DFM principles for manufacture.

Mechanical CAD
Fiverr · 2025 · Client Engineering Package

ImprntMold Pro

Soap Stamp Mould — 14-Sheet Engineering Drawing Package

A complete 14-sheet SolidWorks engineering drawing package for a custom soap stamp mould, commissioned for a client. Covers full assembly (Sheet 2), stamp mold top with key dimensions (Sheet 4), cavity with M5 thread specs and chamfer callouts (Sheet 5), and ejector shaft (Sheet 14). Material: Steel & Brass. 3rd angle projection, Scale 1:1 to 1:5.

CAD + 3D Print
Personal · 2025 · 2D→3D Pipeline

OwlBox Art Series

3D Printed Art Series — From 2D Illustration to Physical Object

A complete 2D-to-3D pipeline project. Took the geometric owl illustration and modelled it as a multi-body SolidWorks assembly — segmenting the design into printable parts, adding a picture frame surround, and physically producing it in multicolour PLA using FDM 3D printing. Demonstrates design decomposition, assembly modelling, and print-ready DfP thinking.

Source: 2D Geometric Illustration SolidWorks Multi-Body Assembly Picture Frame Sub-Assembly FDM 3D Printing — PLA Multicolour — 4 Materials Design for Print (DfP) Pipeline
SolidWorks FDM 3D Printing Multicolour PLA Assembly Modelling 2D→3D
Full-Stack · Python · JavaScript · C

GuardNet IDS

Network Intrusion Detection System — 3-Language Project
github.com/dayoolugbemi/guardnet-ids
Personal / Freelance · 2024

A real-time network intrusion detection system built by Adedayo Olugbemi — born from the Google Cybersecurity Professional Certificate and evolved into a production-grade tool. Three-language architecture: Python handles live packet capture and ML anomaly detection; C firmware runs on ESP32 hardware sensor nodes via UART; React/JS renders a real-time WebSocket dashboard.

guardnet-ids/detector.py — Adedayo Olugbemi
# GuardNet IDS — ML Anomaly Detection Engine
import scapy.all as scapy
from sklearn.ensemble import IsolationForest
import numpy as np, asyncio, websockets, json

model = IsolationForest(contamination=0.05, random_state=42)
CLIENTS = set()

def extract_features(pkt):
    return [
        pkt[scapy.IP].len if scapy.IP in pkt else 0,
        pkt[scapy.TCP].dport if scapy.TCP in pkt else 0,
        pkt[scapy.TCP].flags if scapy.TCP in pkt else 0,
        len(pkt)
    ]

async def broadcast_alert(payload):
    if CLIENTS:
        await asyncio.gather(*[c.send(json.dumps(payload)) for c in CLIENTS])

def on_packet(pkt):
    score = model.decision_function(np.array([extract_features(pkt)]))[0]
    if score < -0.15:
        asyncio.run(broadcast_alert({
            "type": "ANOMALY", "score": round(score, 4),
            "src": pkt[scapy.IP].src, "dst": pkt[scapy.IP].dst
        }))
firmware/sensor_node.c — ESP32 UART Node
/* GuardNet IDS — Adedayo Olugbemi · ESP32 Sensor Node */
#include "driver/uart.h"
#include "esp_wifi.h"
#define UART_PORT    UART_NUM_1
#define BAUD_RATE    115200
#define ALERT_THRESH 120

typedef struct {
    uint32_t pkt_count;
    uint32_t byte_total;
    uint8_t  alert_flag;
} SensorReport;

void send_report(SensorReport *r) {
    char buf[64];
    snprintf(buf, sizeof(buf),
        "{\"pkts\":%lu,\"bytes\":%lu,\"alert\":%d}\n",
        r->pkt_count, r->byte_total, r->alert_flag);
    uart_write_bytes(UART_PORT, buf, strlen(buf));
}
dashboard/src/Dashboard.jsx — React + WebSocket
// GuardNet IDS — Adedayo Olugbemi · Live Alert Dashboard
import { useState, useEffect, useRef } from "react";
import { Line } from "react-chartjs-2";

export default function Dashboard() {
  const [alerts, setAlerts] = useState([]);
  const [scores, setScores] = useState([]);
  const ws = useRef(null);
  useEffect(() => {
    ws.current = new WebSocket("ws://localhost:8765");
    ws.current.onmessage = (e) => {
      const d = JSON.parse(e.data);
      if (d.type === "ANOMALY") {
        setAlerts(p => [d, ...p.slice(0,49)]);
        setScores(p => [...p.slice(-29), d.score]);
      }
    };
    return () => ws.current.close();
  }, []);
  return <Dashboard alerts={alerts} scores={scores} />;
}
